feed.title: Agent Ops Kit — Guides
feed.subtitle: Deep-dive guides on running Claude Code (or any LLM agent CLI) as a scheduled, unattended process.

entry.title: Lint your agent's constitution in CI
entry.summary: The 10 guardrail checks a linter can catch, the GitHub Action that runs them on every PR, and what pattern-matching can't tell you.

entry.title: constitution_lint.py — a heuristic checker for CLAUDE.md-style files
entry.summary: Dependency-free tool that checks a constitution file for the guardrails these guides describe: authority order, injection defense, a real spend limit, an escalation path, secrets handling, ledger discipline, a self-modification guard, honest reporting, the session ritual, and whether referenced state files actually exist on disk.

entry.title: The honest build log: an AI agent runs this business
entry.summary: The live 30-day experiment behind this repo, with the real P&L, what shipped, what failed, and why the human is the bottleneck.

entry.title: Spend rails for autonomous agents
entry.summary: The three numbers that replace "use good judgment about money": a per-transaction autonomy line, an escalation threshold, a lifetime ceiling, plus per-category sub-caps and the ledger discipline that makes a spend cap enforceable instead of theoretical.

entry.title: Writing a constitution file for an autonomous agent
entry.summary: The four things a constitution file has to answer, why a rule you can't test isn't a rule, authority order as the actual security boundary, and the minimal skeleton.

entry.title: File-based memory for scheduled agents
entry.summary: The five state files, the session ritual, write discipline, and the stale-belief failure mode.

entry.title: Security checklist for unattended agents
entry.summary: Prompt-injection defense via authority order, root-owned secrets the agent can't read, spend rails with real numbers, blast-radius containment.

entry.title: Run Claude Code on a schedule
entry.summary: systemd timers vs cron, the lock/timeout/backstop-commit session script, per-slot model selection, timezone traps.

entry.title: A Telegram remote control (STATUS/PAUSE/RESUME)
entry.summary: Outbound alerts worth reading, an append-only directive log, the five commands, why long-polling beats a webhook here.
